summary: Patrick McKenzie recounts his journey from underemployed technical translator in rural Japan to running a profitable software business (Bingo Card Creator) in just 5 hours per week. He details how he leveraged content marketing, Google AdWords Conversion Optimizer, and systematic optimization to grow a hobby project into a real business. The presentation covers his transition from salaryman to full-time entrepreneur, supplemented by consulting income. The tactical half offers five actionable strategies: charge more, build web apps, make your work publicly visible, master SEO, and optimize everything through A/B testing. He emphasizes that multiplicative improvements across the funnel compound dramatically over time.
key_points: A hobby project born from a mailing list request became a real business by applying SEO, AdWords, and content marketing systematically over years
Google's Conversion Optimizer automated the tedious work of banning spam sites and turbocharged ad spend on converting traffic, particularly during seasonal spikes like Halloween
Scalable content creation — partially automated, partially outsourced — generated nearly a thousand SEO-optimized pages that delivered free organic traffic
Converting from desktop app to web application roughly doubled sales due to improved conversion rates at every stage of the funnel
All factors in software business success are multiplicative: small compounding improvements across the funnel yield outsized results over time
money_quotes: Most engineers severely undercharge for their products.

Almost all SaaS firms find that they make huge portions of their revenue from the topmost plan which is bought by people spending other people's money, but instead of optimizing for this we optimize for charging 'fair' prices as determined by other software developers who won't pay for the service anyway.
Businesses create value with almost everything they do. The lion's share of the value is, like an iceberg, below the waterline: it cannot be seen from anywhere outside the business.
Did you write software not core to your line of business? Great, OSS it. Get the credit.
If you increase conversions to the trial by 10% and conversions to sale by 10%, your sales go up by 21%, because 1.1 * 1.1 = 1.21.
What real company offers 70% raises per year just for doing an A/B test every week and collecting a +5% improvement on one out of every four?
I budgeted $60 (one video game) and one week to rewriting and productizing it, and set myself a goal: some day, after months of work, I wanted to make $200 in sales a month.
Gifu is the Kansas of Japan, with more rice and less white people.
key_insight: A solo software business can be grown to substantial revenue in just 5 hours per week by systematically applying content marketing, conversion optimization, and automation — treating every improvement as a multiplier rather than an addition.
